description:
You are acting as an infrastructure engineer who has just finished an automated
    provisioning run.  
    The very last step of the playbook is to drop a simple “success-marker” file so
    that other tools can quickly decide whether the host is already configured.

    Requirement
    1. Create a file whose full path is **/home/user/provisioned.flag**.
    2. The file must contain **exactly** the single word  
       ```
       DONE
       ```  
       followed by a single Unix newline character (`\n`).  
       • No leading or trailing spaces.  
       • No additional lines.
    3. Accomplish this with one single terminal command (you may compose several
       shell operators on the same line, but everything must be submitted in a
       single interactive prompt).

    Verification rules the grader will apply
    • The file **/home/user/provisioned.flag** must exist.  
    • `wc -l` on the file must return `1`.  
    • `cat /home/user/provisioned.flag` must output the two characters  
      `D`, `O`, `N`, `E`, then a newline and nothing else.  

    No other output or files are required.  Make sure the command you choose creates
    the file with the precise contents in one go.
truth:
The grader will test:
    1. `[ -f /home/user/provisioned.flag ]` is true.
    2. `stat --format=%s /home/user/provisioned.flag` returns **5** (four letters + newline).
    3. `cat /home/user/provisioned.flag` returns exactly `"DONE\n"` (byte sequence 44 4F 4E 45 0A).
name: task_000000_0b8658d4
